_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to describe how different text types use specific language features and are organised into characteristic stages and phases to achieve their purpose.

_source.w: We are learning to describe how different text types use specific language features and are organised into characteristic stages and phases to achieve their purpose.
y: Understanding the internal structure of text types helps us read and write them more skillfully.
Recognising stages and phases gives us a framework for creating well-organised texts.
This knowledge helps us across all our subjects where different text types are required.
eg: We map the stages of a persuasive text — thesis, arguments, reinforcement of position.
We identify the phases within a narrative — orientation, complication, series of events, resolution.
We compare the stages of an explanation text with those of a procedure text.
We discuss how the language features in each stage serve the text's overall purpose.
We use our knowledge of text stages to plan our own writing before we begin.
We analyse a text that does not follow its expected structure and discuss why it is less effective.
